---
title: "Multi-Scale Residual Covariance"
subtitle: "Experiment exp_multiscale_residual_covariance"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
---

## Overview

Compares single-scale vs multi-scale Matérn NNGP residual covariance on Big Sur daily streamflow.
The multi-scale variant uses two sequential Matérn scales (short + long) in a stacked NNGP whitening objective.

**Config:** K=4, m=20, ν=1.5, maxiter=202, seed=42, Gaussian kernels.

## Test Performance

| Model | sim CRPS | sim NSE | sim RMSE | krig CRPS | krig NSE | krig RMSE |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Single-scale (SWR-GP) | 0.644 | 0.660 | 2.463 | 0.394 | 0.823 | 1.776 |
| Multi-scale | 0.831 | 0.424 | — | 0.533 | 0.774 | — |

**Improvement (multi-scale − single-scale):**

| Metric | Delta |
|---|---|
| sim CRPS | +0.187 (worse) |
| krig CRPS | +0.139 (worse) |
| sim NSE | −0.236 (worse) |
| krig NSE | −0.049 (worse) |

## Conclusion

Multi-scale residual covariance does not improve performance on this dataset.
The added flexibility in residual structure degrades both simulation and kriging metrics,
suggesting the single-scale Matérn is already a good fit for the temporal dependence here.

## Notes

Best single-scale parameters: θ = (σ²=0.413, φ=12.510, ν=1.5, τ²=0.012).
